profile.behavior: Usually forages from high perches, making short sallies after insects and returning to a perch; spends little time on the ground.
field_marks: Large, lean flycatcher with a broad-shouldered look, large head, and subtle peaked crest.
Brownish-gray head, throat, and breast contrast with a bright lemon-yellow belly.
Thick, straight dark bill, often with a paler base to the lower mandible.
Olive- to reddish-brown upperparts with pale-edged wing feathers.
Rufous-orange flashes in the wings and a long, rufous-brown tail.
Dark legs and feet; sexes alike in plumage.
profile.habitat: Open broadleaf or mixed woodlands, woodland edges, clearings, hedgerows, and other tree-rich habitats; typically uses the upper canopy and nests in cavities.

sources.title: Great Crested Flycatcher Identification, All About Birds
Great Crested Flycatcher Life History, All About Birds
Great Crested Flycatcher, Audubon Field Guide

findings: Passed: the illustration shows one head, one straight beak, two legs and feet, one tail, and the anatomically expected pair of wings (the far wing is occluded).

Species identity is well supported by the brownish-gray head and chest, bright lemon-yellow belly, broad-shouldered large-headed profile, straight dark bill, pale-edged brown wings with rufous primary flashes, and long rufous-brown tail; these agree with the supplied reference photos and both live field guides.
The crest is rendered somewhat more conspicuously swept-up than the usually subtle/bushy crest in the reference photos, but remains within an acceptable scientific-illustration interpretation.
All visible factual text is supported. The plate's 17–21 cm, 6.7–8.3 in, and 27–40 g values match All About Birds; its 33–38 cm, 13–15 in wingspan matches Audubon. No material text error found.
No place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail appears on the candidate.
